name: Kiel (United States)

colors.symbolism: the upper portion of the heraldic field, recalling the colours of Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein
the lower portion of the field
heraldic accents and decorative ribbons in the coat of arms
ornamentation and the boat/key device of Kiel
outlines and detailing in the coat of arms

description: A heraldic banner divided horizontally into blue above and white below, charged with the city's coat of arms — a shield bearing a stylised boat or key surrounded by ribbons, leaves and red-and-gold ornament — across the centre.
history: Kiel, Wisconsin, was founded by mid-nineteenth-century German immigrants who named the new community after Kiel in Schleswig-Holstein. The municipal flag adapts the heraldry of the German parent city.

features.symbolism: A heraldic banner-style flag bearing the city's coat of arms across the field, evoking the Kiel coat of arms of the German seaport for which the Wisconsin city is named.

features.role: Coat of arms of the City of Kiel, Wisconsin
features.symbolism: A heraldic shield based on the arms of Kiel, Germany — the namesake seaport on the Baltic — adapted as the civic emblem of Kiel, Wisconsin, founded by German settlers in the nineteenth century.
